channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Humboldt Research Fellowship 2026–2027 in Germany: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Postdoctoral and Experienced Researchers Worldwide
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Humboldt Research Fellowship offered by the Alexander von Humboldt Foundation is one of the most prestigious international research fellowships available to scholars across the globe. This program provides highly qualified postdoctoral and experienced researchers from all academic disciplines with the opportunity to conduct independent research in Germany in collaboration with leading research institutions and […]

The post Humboldt Research Fellowship 2026–2027 in Germany: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Postdoctoral and Experienced Researchers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Humboldt Research Fellowship offered by the Alexander von Humboldt Foundation is one of the most prestigious international research fellowships available to scholars across the globe. This program provides highly qualified postdoctoral and experienced researchers from all academic disciplines with the opportunity to conduct independent research in Germany in collaboration with leading research institutions and host scholars.

The fellowship is part of the Global Minds Initiative Germany, funded by the Federal Ministry of Research, Technology and Space, and aims to attract outstanding researchers from around the world to strengthen global scientific collaboration and academic exchange.

Through this prestigious fellowship, scholars gain access to Germany’s cutting-edge research environment, world-class laboratories, academic networks, and long-term collaboration opportunities with German institutions.

Researchers who receive the Humboldt Research Fellowship join a prestigious global community known as the “Humboldtians”, which includes thousands of distinguished scientists, Nobel laureates, and leading academics.

About the Alexander von Humboldt Foundation

The Alexander von Humboldt Foundation is a globally respected organization dedicated to promoting international academic cooperation and scientific excellence.

The foundation supports researchers at different stages of their careers and builds long-term academic partnerships between Germany and the global research community.

Through the Humboldt Research Fellowship, the foundation provides:

  • Individual research sponsorship

  • Financial support for living and research expenses

  • Academic networking opportunities

  • Long-term alumni support

  • Collaboration with German host institutions

The foundation also strongly promotes diversity, equal opportunity, and gender balance, encouraging applications from female researchers and scholars from underrepresented groups.

Overview of the Humboldt Research Fellowship

The Humboldt Research Fellowship allows international researchers to conduct independent research projects in Germany in collaboration with a host at a German research institution of their choice.

Applicants can choose their host institution freely, provided that a host professor or research supervisor agrees to support their research proposal and confirms that the necessary research facilities are available.

The fellowship provides financial support for research stays lasting between 6 and 24 months, depending on the applicant’s career stage.

The program supports researchers from all disciplines, including:

  • Natural sciences

  • Engineering

  • Life sciences

  • Social sciences

  • Humanities

  • Interdisciplinary fields

This flexibility allows scholars to pursue innovative research ideas and develop global collaborations.

Fellowship Categories

The Humboldt Research Fellowship offers two primary fellowship categories depending on the applicant’s academic career stage.

1. Humboldt Research Fellowship for Postdoctoral Researchers

This category is designed for early-career researchers who have recently completed their doctorate.

Fellowship Benefits for Postdoctoral Researchers

Postdoctoral fellows receive:

  • Monthly fellowship of €3,000

  • Research stay in Germany lasting 6–24 months

  • Possibility to divide the fellowship into up to three research stays within three years

  • Access to academic mentorship and research facilities

This fellowship helps early-career researchers develop their academic careers and build international collaborations.

2. Humboldt Research Fellowship for Experienced Researchers

This fellowship category targets established scholars who have advanced in their academic careers and have strong research records.

Fellowship Benefits for Experienced Researchers

Experienced researchers receive:

  • Monthly fellowship of €3,600

  • Research stay lasting 6–18 months

  • Opportunity to divide the fellowship into up to three research stays within three years

These fellowships support senior academics in expanding research collaborations and strengthening international partnerships.

What the Humboldt Research Fellowship Offers

The Humboldt Research Fellowship provides extensive support to ensure that fellows can focus fully on their research projects.

Key benefits include:

1. Financial Support

The fellowship provides generous financial assistance, including:

  • Monthly stipend (€3,000–€3,600 depending on career stage)

  • Travel allowances

  • Health insurance subsidies

  • Research cost subsidies for host institutions

  • Additional financial support for families

2. Language Training

Before beginning their research stay, fellows and their partners may attend intensive German language courses.

This language training helps fellows integrate into German academic and social environments.

3. Family Support

Additional support is available for fellows with families, including:

  • Family allowances for children and partners

  • Financial support for childcare expenses

  • Assistance with relocation

4. Research Flexibility

The fellowship offers significant flexibility, allowing fellows to:

  • Choose their own host institution

  • Determine the start date of their research stay

  • Divide their fellowship period into multiple research visits

Alumni Support and Long-Term Opportunities

The Humboldt Foundation maintains long-term relationships with its fellows even after their research stays end.

Fellows become part of the Humboldt Alumni Network, which offers:

  • Return fellowships

  • Funding for research visits to Germany

  • Participation in academic conferences

  • Networking events and collaborations

These programs ensure that researchers maintain strong connections with German institutions and continue international collaboration throughout their careers.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet several eligibility criteria to apply for the Humboldt Research Fellowship.

1. Academic Qualification

Applicants must have completed a doctoral degree or equivalent academic qualification.

For Postdoctoral Researchers

  • Doctorate completed within the last four years

  • Strong publication record in internationally recognized journals

For Experienced Researchers

  • Doctorate completed within the last twelve years

  • Extensive publication record

  • Demonstrated academic leadership (e.g., assistant professor, lecturer, research group leader)

In fields where doctorates are uncommon, candidates may apply up to 16 years after completing a master’s degree if they have an appropriate research publication record.

2. Research Host in Germany

Applicants must secure a host researcher in Germany who:

  • Provides a detailed statement supporting the research project

  • Confirms that research facilities are available

The host institution plays a crucial role in facilitating the fellow’s research activities.

3. Academic Excellence

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Outstanding academic achievements

  • Innovative research proposals

  • Strong academic potential

  • High-quality publications

4. Language Skills

Depending on the discipline, applicants may need:

  • English language proficiency

  • German language skills if required by the research project

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit several documents when applying online.

Required materials include:

  1. Curriculum Vitae (maximum two pages)

  2. Research outline (maximum five pages)

  3. Complete list of publications

  4. List of selected key publications

  5. Copies of key publications

  6. Doctoral certificate or confirmation of doctoral completion

  7. Language certificates if required

  8. Two expert reviews submitted by academic referees

  9. Host institution statement confirming research support

  10. Publisher acceptance notifications (if applicable)

Applications missing required documents may be formally rejected without review.

Application Schedule for 2026

The Humboldt Foundation introduced a structured application schedule with three calls per year.

Call Opening Date Selection Meeting Earliest Fellowship Start
15 March 2026 November 2026 February 2027
15 July 2026 March 2027 May 2027
15 November 2026 July 2027 September 2027

Each call will accept a maximum of 800 applications.

Once the limit is reached, the application round will close.

Selection Procedure

Applications are evaluated through a rigorous peer-review process conducted by a selection committee of 60–70 international researchers.

The selection process includes:

  1. Formal eligibility verification (4–8 weeks)

  2. Peer review and academic evaluation (3–6 months)

  3. Selection committee meetings held three times annually

  4. Final decision and notification to applicants

Evaluation criteria include:

  • Academic achievements and career trajectory

  • Originality and significance of research proposal

  • Quality of publications

  • Feasibility of research project

  • Future academic potential

Approximately 20–25% of applications are approved.

Decision Notification

Applicants and hosts receive official notification approximately five weeks after the selection meeting.

Successful applicants receive fellowship documentation and instructions for starting their research stay.

Rejected applicants may request feedback and may reapply after improving their proposals.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted online through the Alexander von Humboldt Foundation portal.

Applicants must ensure that all required documents are uploaded before submitting their application.

Incomplete applications may result in rejection or delayed processing.

Learn more about this Research Fellowship HERE.

Discover more global Fellowship opportunities HERE.

The post Humboldt Research Fellowship 2026–2027 in Germany: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Postdoctoral and Experienced Researchers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GreenShift Ambassador Program 2026 in Nepal: Empowering Youth to Lead the Circular Economy Transition (Apply by 30 March 2026)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The GreenShift Ambassador Program 2026 is an exciting youth leadership initiative designed to empower young changemakers in Nepal to take an active role in promoting sustainability, environmental responsibility, and the transition toward a circular economy. The program forms part of the GreenShift Nepal project—Circularity of Plastic Waste for Net-Zero Carbon Nepal, which is funded by […]

The post GreenShift Ambassador Program 2026 in Nepal: Empowering Youth to Lead the Circular Economy Transition (Apply by 30 March 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The GreenShift Ambassador Program 2026 is an exciting youth leadership initiative designed to empower young changemakers in Nepal to take an active role in promoting sustainability, environmental responsibility, and the transition toward a circular economy. The program forms part of the GreenShift Nepal project—Circularity of Plastic Waste for Net-Zero Carbon Nepal, which is funded by the European Union and implemented through a consortium led by CREASION, in partnership with Restless Development Nepal and Youth Innovation Lab (YI-LAB).

This volunteer-based initiative seeks to select dedicated youth leaders from Bagmati, Lumbini, and Madhesh provinces who are passionate about climate action, community engagement, and sustainable development. Selected ambassadors will play a key role in raising awareness about responsible plastic waste management, circular economy principles, and environmental stewardship.

The program will run for three months from May to July 2026, during which participants will engage in training programs, community outreach initiatives, sustainability campaigns, and policy advocacy efforts related to environmental protection and plastic waste management.

About the GreenShift Nepal Project

The GreenShift: Circularity of Plastic Waste for Net-Zero Carbon Nepal initiative is a multi-year environmental program designed to promote a just transition toward a circular economy in Nepal.

According to the project overview provided in the program document, GreenShift Nepal is a four-year project implemented in nine municipalities, with three municipalities each in Bagmati, Madhesh, and Lumbini provinces.

The project works collaboratively with multiple stakeholders, including:

  • Waste management enterprises

  • Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs)

  • Civil society organizations

  • Waste workers

  • Youth groups

  • Educational institutions

  • Government bodies at local, provincial, and national levels

The primary goal is to promote circular economy principles in plastic waste management, ensuring that materials are reused, recycled, and repurposed rather than disposed of in landfills or polluting ecosystems.

By engaging communities, businesses, and policymakers, GreenShift Nepal aims to contribute significantly to the country’s net-zero carbon ambitions and environmental sustainability goals.

Background of the GreenShift Ambassador Initiative

The GreenShift Ambassador program is an integral component of the broader #GreenShift campaign, which seeks to mobilize youth leadership in driving sustainable environmental practices.

Currently in its third year, the campaign has already supported numerous youth-led initiatives across Nepal.

The ambassador program specifically focuses on empowering young people to:

  • Promote environmental awareness

  • Advocate for responsible plastic waste management

  • Encourage community participation in sustainability initiatives

  • Support the transition to a circular economy

The program also aims to expand the GreenShift campaign’s reach into Bagmati, Lumbini, and Madhesh provinces, strengthening youth engagement and leadership in these regions.

Through this initiative, the project aims to educate, empower, and mobilize youth leaders, ensuring they become key contributors to Nepal’s sustainable development journey.

Objectives of the GreenShift Ambassador Program

The primary objective of the GreenShift Ambassador initiative is to identify and train youth leaders who can drive sustainability-focused initiatives in their communities.

Key objectives include:

  1. Selecting dedicated youth ambassadors from the target provinces.

  2. Promoting environmental awareness and sustainable practices.

  3. Engaging local communities in plastic waste reduction and recycling initiatives.

  4. Encouraging youth participation in environmental advocacy and policy dialogue.

  5. Strengthening collaboration between youth, government institutions, and civil society organizations.

Through these objectives, the initiative seeks to foster a strong sense of ownership and responsibility among young leaders.

GreenShift Ambassadors will serve as community champions who lead sustainability activities, collaborate with stakeholders, and mobilize citizens to adopt environmentally responsible behaviors.

Impact of the GreenShift Ambassador Program

The program aims to generate a ripple effect of environmental awareness and proactive engagement throughout communities in Nepal.

Selected ambassadors will play a pivotal role in organizing sustainability initiatives such as:

  • Educational workshops

  • Environmental awareness campaigns

  • Plastic waste reduction programs

  • Community clean-up drives

  • Tree planting activities

  • Youth-led advocacy initiatives

These efforts are expected to contribute not only to immediate environmental improvements but also to long-term behavioral change within communities.

By bridging the gap between policy frameworks and grassroots implementation, GreenShift Ambassadors will help ensure that sustainability policies translate into real-world action.

The ultimate vision of the program is to nurture a generation of environmentally responsible citizens who are committed to building a greener and more sustainable Nepal.

Program Duration

The GreenShift Ambassador Program will run for three months, starting in May 2026 and ending in July 2026.

During this period, participants will:

  • Attend specialized training sessions

  • Participate in workshops and knowledge-sharing sessions

  • Organize community engagement activities

  • Collaborate with stakeholders to promote sustainability initiatives

Active participation throughout the program is expected from all selected ambassadors.

Application Process

The selection of GreenShift Ambassadors follows a structured multi-stage process designed to identify highly motivated youth leaders.

1. Announcement of the Call for Green Ambassadors

The program begins with the official launch of the call for applications through CREASION’s social media platforms and the GreenShift website.

The campaign aims to attract passionate youth who are interested in contributing to environmental sustainability and community development.

2. Application Submission

Interested candidates must submit their applications through an Online Application Form.

Applicants are required to provide:

  • Personal details

  • Motivation for joining the program

  • Experience in community activities or leadership roles

The application process allows the selection committee to identify candidates who demonstrate strong commitment, creativity, and leadership potential.

3. Selection Process

After the application period closes, all submissions will be carefully reviewed.

Candidates will be assessed based on:

  • Commitment to environmental sustainability

  • Leadership potential

  • Innovative ideas for community engagement

  • Alignment with the objectives of the GreenShift campaign

From the pool of applicants, three ambassadors will be selected from each of the three provinces.

4. Announcement of Selected Ambassadors

Selected ambassadors will be publicly announced through:

  • CREASION’s official social media platforms

  • The GreenShift website

This announcement will highlight the selected youth leaders and celebrate their commitment to sustainability.

5. Onboarding and Training

Following selection, ambassadors will participate in a comprehensive onboarding and training program.

The training will equip them with:

  • Leadership skills

  • Project management knowledge

  • Community engagement strategies

  • Environmental advocacy techniques

Ambassadors will also receive guidance on organizing campaigns and collaborating with stakeholders.

Responsibilities of GreenShift Ambassadors

Selected ambassadors will be responsible for leading sustainability initiatives within their communities.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Leading community campaigns on environmental awareness

  • Promoting youth engagement in sustainability initiatives

  • Initiating sustainability activities in colleges and universities

  • Advocating for circular economy practices related to plastic waste

  • Supporting evidence-based and data-driven policy advocacy

  • Collaborating with fellow ambassadors to share best practices

  • Building networks of youth changemakers across regions

Through these activities, ambassadors will contribute directly to the success of the GreenShift campaign.

Benefits of Becoming a GreenShift Ambassador

Participants in the GreenShift Ambassador program will gain valuable skills, experiences, and recognition.

1. Capacity-Building Trainings

Ambassadors will participate in specialized training sessions focused on:

  • Leadership development

  • Project management

  • Community engagement

  • Circular economy principles

  • Plastic waste management

  • Environmental and social safeguarding standards

Participants will also receive Training of Trainers (ToT) sessions, enabling them to educate and inspire other youth in their communities.

2. Hands-On Environmental Experience

Ambassadors will gain practical experience in implementing sustainability projects and community campaigns.

This hands-on experience can help participants explore future career opportunities in the environmental sector.

3. Certificates and Letters of Recommendation

Upon successfully completing the program, ambassadors will receive:

  • Official certificates

  • Letters of recommendation

These credentials can significantly strengthen participants’ professional profiles.

4. GreenShift Merchandise

Ambassadors will receive branded GreenShift merchandise such as:

  • T-shirts

  • Tote bags

These items help promote the campaign and recognize participants’ contributions.

5. Recognition and Public Exposure

Ambassadors’ activities and achievements will be featured on:

  • CREASION’s website

  • Social media platforms

  • Media outlets

Outstanding ambassadors may also receive coverage in local and national media, increasing their visibility and influence.

Number of Ambassadors Selected

A total of nine GreenShift Ambassadors will be selected:

  • Bagmati Province – 3 ambassadors

  • Lumbini Province – 3 ambassadors

  • Madhesh Province – 3 ambassadors

This distribution ensures equal representation across the targeted provinces.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Age between 18 and 25 years

  • Educational background of +2 or currently pursuing a bachelor’s degree

  • Availability to participate in the program from May to July 2026

  • Demonstrated interest in community development, environmental sustainability, or climate action

  • Prior volunteer experience is considered an advantage but not mandatory

  • Strong communication skills in Nepali and English, both written and spoken

The program welcomes motivated young individuals who are eager to create positive change in their communities.

Application Deadline

Applications for the GreenShift Ambassador Program must be submitted before 30 March 2026.

Interested candidates should apply as early as possible to ensure their application is reviewed.

Read more about the GreenShift Ambassador Program 2026 in Nepal HERE.

Click HERE to discover more other global fellowship programmes on OFY. 

The post GreenShift Ambassador Program 2026 in Nepal: Empowering Youth to Lead the Circular Economy Transition (Apply by 30 March 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for Italian Government Scholarships 2026–2027 | Study in Italy MAECI Funding for Foreign Students
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Italian government has officially opened applications for the Italian Government Scholarships for the 2026–2027 academic year, offering international students and Italians Residing Abroad (IRE) the opportunity to pursue studies in Italy. The program is funded and administered by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation (MAECI) and aims to strengthen international academic collaboration […]

The post Apply for Italian Government Scholarships 2026–2027 | Study in Italy MAECI Funding for Foreign Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Italian government has officially opened applications for the Italian Government Scholarships for the 2026–2027 academic year, offering international students and Italians Residing Abroad (IRE) the opportunity to pursue studies in Italy. The program is funded and administered by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation (MAECI) and aims to strengthen international academic collaboration while promoting Italian culture, language, and innovation.

These scholarships are designed to support students who wish to study in Italy and contribute to academic exchange between Italy and the rest of the world.

About the Italian Government Scholarship Program

The scholarship initiative is part of Italy’s broader effort to promote cultural diplomacy and global academic partnerships. Through the program, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation seeks to:

  • Strengthen international cooperation in cultural, scientific, and technological fields

  • Promote the Italian economic and research ecosystem globally

  • Increase global awareness of the Italian language and culture

  • Support academic exchange between Italy and partner countries

The scholarship is offered under the provisions of Law 288/55, including subsequent amendments and updates.

Academic Year Covered

The scholarship applies to the 2026–2027 academic year and supports studies exclusively at institutions located in Italy.

Students who receive the scholarship will attend approved academic programs within the Italian higher education system.

What the Scholarship Covers

The scholarship supports students who wish to study in Italy, and it applies specifically to courses physically attended in the country. This means the scholarship cannot be used for online programs or studies outside Italy.

Programs eligible under the scholarship typically include:

  • Master’s degree programs

  • Higher education courses

  • Research programs

  • Italian language and culture courses

Exact details about the supported programs are provided in the official call for applications.

Why Study in Italy?

Italy is home to some of the oldest and most prestigious universities in the world, offering high-quality education across a wide range of disciplines including arts, engineering, science, economics, and international relations.

Studying in Italy allows students to:

  • Experience a rich cultural and historical environment

  • Access globally recognized academic institutions

  • Build international academic and professional networks

  • Improve proficiency in the Italian language

  • Explore Europe while studying

Eligible Applicants

The scholarship is open to two main categories of applicants:

1. Foreign Students

Students from eligible countries who wish to pursue higher education in Italy.

2. Italians Residing Abroad (IRE)

Italian citizens who permanently live outside Italy and wish to pursue academic programs in the country.

The complete list of eligible countries and territories can be found in the official call for applications on the Study in Italy portal.

Where to Find the Official Call

Applicants should carefully review the official scholarship announcement and eligibility requirements before applying.

The call for applications and the list of eligible beneficiary countries are available on the official portal:

Study in Italy Scholarship Portal

Application Process

Applications for the scholarship must be submitted online through the official Study in Italy portal.

Applicants cannot submit applications via email or through other platforms. Only submissions made through the official portal will be considered valid.

To apply:

  1. Visit the Study in Italy website.

  2. Create or log into your account.

  3. Complete the scholarship application form.

  4. Upload all required documents.

  5. Submit the application before the deadline.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the Italian Government Scholarships 2026–2027 is:

26 March 2026 at 2:00 PM (Italian time).

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid technical issues or last-minute delays.

The Italian Government Scholarships for the 2026–2027 academic year provide a valuable opportunity for international students and Italians living abroad to pursue academic programs in Italy. With a strong focus on cultural exchange, research collaboration, and educational development, the program continues to strengthen Italy’s role as a global destination for higher education.

Interested candidates should review the official call carefully and submit their applications before the 26 March 2026 deadline.

Discover more similar opportunities by clicking Here

The post Apply for Italian Government Scholarships 2026–2027 | Study in Italy MAECI Funding for Foreign Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Emergency Funding for Feminist Groups in Lebanon: Doria Feminist Fund Responds to 2026 Humanitarian Crisis
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The humanitarian situation in Lebanon has rapidly deteriorated following the escalation of regional conflict in early March 2026. Large-scale displacement, collapsing health systems, widespread insecurity, and severe humanitarian funding shortages have created an urgent need for immediate intervention. In response to this crisis, the Doria Feminist Fund (DFF) has announced an emergency response initiative aimed […]

The post Emergency Funding for Feminist Groups in Lebanon: Doria Feminist Fund Responds to 2026 Humanitarian Crisis appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The humanitarian situation in Lebanon has rapidly deteriorated following the escalation of regional conflict in early March 2026. Large-scale displacement, collapsing health systems, widespread insecurity, and severe humanitarian funding shortages have created an urgent need for immediate intervention. In response to this crisis, the Doria Feminist Fund (DFF) has announced an emergency response initiative aimed at supporting feminist organizations and collectives working on the frontlines of humanitarian relief in Lebanon.

Through its Emergency Response Grantmaking program, DFF will provide flexible financial support and operational accompaniment to grassroots feminist groups responding directly to the crisis and supporting vulnerable populations affected by the conflict.

This article provides a detailed overview of the current humanitarian context in Lebanon, the populations most affected, and the structure and objectives of the emergency response grants being implemented by the Doria Feminist Fund.

The Escalating Crisis in Lebanon

Regional Conflict and the Triggering Events

The current crisis in Lebanon is part of a wider regional escalation that began in late February 2026. Coordinated military strikes by the United States and Israel targeted Iran, resulting in the death of Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. In retaliation, Iran launched extensive missile and drone attacks across several Gulf countries.

Shortly afterward, on March 2, Hezbollah launched precision-guided missiles toward northern Israel. Israel responded by launching large-scale military operations targeting Hezbollah infrastructure across several parts of Lebanon, including Beirut’s southern suburbs, the Bekaa Valley, and southern regions of the country.

The Lebanese government subsequently banned all Hezbollah security and military activities and demanded the surrender of weapons. However, hostilities continued to escalate across the country.

Humanitarian Impact of the Conflict

Casualties and Widespread Displacement

Within the first eight days of the conflict, the humanitarian situation worsened dramatically.

By March 11, 2026:

  • 634 people had been killed.

  • 1,586 individuals had been injured.

  • Over 816,700 people had registered as internally displaced.

  • Approximately 125,800 displaced individuals were sheltering in 590 collective shelters.

  • More than one million people were affected by deteriorating humanitarian conditions.

The displacement crisis has expanded rapidly, increasing more than twenty-five times within a single week. Many families have been forced to move repeatedly due to continuous evacuation orders issued across multiple regions.

Evacuation orders initially targeted dozens of villages but eventually expanded to cover the entire area south of the Litani River as well as densely populated areas of Beirut’s southern suburbs, home to roughly 700,000 residents. These orders have been frequently revised or renewed, preventing displaced populations from returning home.

Collapsing Infrastructure and Public Services

Healthcare System Under Severe Strain

Lebanon’s healthcare system is facing an unprecedented crisis. Hospitals and medical facilities have been directly affected by attacks and ongoing hostilities.

As of March 10:

  • Five hospitals had closed.

  • Forty-eight primary healthcare centers were no longer operational.

  • Seven additional facilities were functioning only in emergency mode.

  • Fourteen health workers had been killed.

  • Twenty-four healthcare workers had been injured.

The remaining healthcare facilities are overwhelmed by trauma cases while patients with chronic illnesses struggle to receive routine treatment. Pregnant and lactating women also face significant challenges in accessing maternal healthcare.

Reports have also documented attacks on healthcare facilities and personnel, raising serious concerns about potential violations of international humanitarian law.

Education Disruption

The education system has also been severely affected.

  • 328 schools have been converted into shelters for displaced families.

  • This has disrupted the education of more than 104,200 children.

  • An estimated 181,000 displaced children are of school age.

The Ministry of Education has temporarily closed all institutions and introduced alternative learning programs, but many displaced families lack internet access or electricity, making remote learning difficult or impossible.

Shelter Conditions and Living Standards

The rapid rise in displacement has created overcrowded and under-resourced shelters.

Despite distributions of mattresses, blankets, sleeping mats, and pillows, many shelters still lack sufficient supplies. Children, older persons, and people with disabilities often sleep without proper bedding.

Shelter conditions have also raised protection concerns. Women and girls frequently report feeling unsafe due to overcrowding, lack of privacy, and inadequate sanitation facilities. Accessibility remains a major issue, as most shelters are not designed to accommodate people with disabilities.

Aid distribution has also been uneven in some areas, which has contributed to growing social tensions among displaced populations.

Severe Funding Gap in Humanitarian Response

Humanitarian organizations are struggling to maintain emergency operations due to a significant funding shortfall.

UNICEF has estimated that approximately $48.29 million is required to support humanitarian response efforts over a three-month period. However, only $3.24 million has been secured so far, leaving a funding gap of 93 percent.

This shortage threatens the continuity of essential services such as healthcare, food distribution, sanitation support, and child protection programming. Without immediate funding, humanitarian agencies may be forced to scale back operations despite rapidly growing needs.

Vulnerable Populations Facing Heightened Risks

Certain groups face disproportionate impacts from the crisis.

Women and Girls

Women and girls face heightened risks of gender-based violence due to overcrowded shelter environments and lack of privacy. Around 4,000 households are currently headed by women who are responsible for supporting families without partners.

Access to menstrual hygiene supplies remains limited, and maternal healthcare services are severely constrained due to hospital closures.

Children

Children represent one of the most vulnerable groups in the crisis.

Many children have experienced trauma after witnessing violence, losing family members, or being displaced multiple times. Psychosocial distress among children is increasing, while the availability of mental health support remains limited.

The number of unaccompanied or separated children identified so far is likely significantly underreported.

Persons with Disabilities and Older Persons

Persons with disabilities face severe accessibility barriers in shelters and sanitation facilities. Many have lost essential assistive devices during displacement and lack access to replacement services.

Older persons also face significant challenges evacuating conflict zones due to mobility limitations and chronic health conditions. Many older households are currently unable to access shelters or medical care.

Refugees, Migrants, and Stateless Persons

Lebanon hosts large refugee and migrant populations who face additional barriers to assistance.

Syrian refugees, Palestinian refugees, migrant workers, and stateless individuals often struggle to access shelters or humanitarian services due to legal restrictions, documentation issues, and language barriers.

Approximately 37,000 Syrians reportedly crossed back into Syria despite ongoing conflict there, highlighting the severity of the crisis inside Lebanon.

LGBTQI+ Communities

LGBTQI+ individuals remain largely invisible in official humanitarian planning and response mechanisms. Overcrowded shelters with strict gender segregation pose significant risks of harassment, discrimination, and violence.

The absence of inclusive services, trained staff, and protective protocols leaves this population particularly vulnerable.

Doria Feminist Fund Emergency Response Initiative

To address the urgent needs created by the crisis, the Doria Feminist Fund has activated its Emergency Response Grantmaking program.

Originally launched in 2023, this initiative provides flexible funding to feminist organizations and collectives responding to unexpected crises. The program focuses on strengthening feminist movements while supporting grassroots humanitarian responses.

The emergency grants are designed to ensure that frontline feminist groups can respond quickly and effectively to the needs of their communities.

Purpose of the Emergency Response Grants

The grants will support feminist organizations in Lebanon that have adapted their work to respond to the humanitarian crisis.

Funding may be used for several types of emergency interventions, including:

  • Emergency community support such as shelter, relocation assistance, medical care, and livelihood support.

  • Expansion of organizational activities to include humanitarian assistance, conflict transformation initiatives, and mutual aid.

  • Organizational sustainability during crisis conditions, including replacing lost equipment, covering salaries, or securing temporary office space.

  • Physical and emotional wellbeing support for activists and communities, including psychosocial support services.

These flexible funding mechanisms allow organizations to adapt their work according to rapidly changing crisis conditions.

Grant Size and Funding Structure

The Doria Feminist Fund will provide emergency response grants of up to $25,000 per organization.

Key features of the funding include:

  • Grant duration: Up to six months

  • Eligibility: Current and former DFF grantee partners in Lebanon

  • Application process: Invitation-based

  • Review process: Rolling evaluation by the DFF grants team

The fund is designed to support feminist collectives that are already responding to the crisis and delivering services within their communities.

Rapid Response Funding Mechanism

Due to the volatility of Lebanon’s financial system, the emergency response grants follow a rapid disbursement model.

Under this system:

  • Organizations receive feedback on their applications within two to three working days.

  • Grant funds are disbursed within two to three working days after the agreement is signed.

This fast-track funding model ensures that resources reach frontline organizations quickly during periods of acute crisis.

Additional Support for Grantee Partners

Beyond financial support, DFF will provide direct accompaniment to partner organizations to strengthen their ability to operate in a conflict environment.

Support includes:

Financial Management Support

Guidance on managing emergency funds and maintaining financial systems under crisis conditions.

Digital Security Training

Workshops designed to help organizations protect communications and sensitive information during conflict situations.

Psychosocial Support for Activists

Facilitated sessions based on DFF’s Psychosocial Support Manual to promote collective care, wellbeing, and resilience within feminist movements.

Strengthening Feminist Crisis Response in Lebanon

Since 2024, the Doria Feminist Fund has supported feminist organizations across Lebanon responding to humanitarian crises. Past support has included assistance for displaced communities, migrant workers, LGBTQIA+ individuals, and refugee populations.

Feminist groups have played a crucial role in providing:

  • Emergency shelters for women and children

  • Cash assistance for vulnerable migrant workers

  • Relocation support for LGBTQ communities

  • Food, bedding, and hygiene supplies for displaced populations

  • Mental health and emotional support programs for activists and affected communities

Through its emergency response grants, DFF aims to strengthen these locally led efforts and ensure that marginalized populations remain supported during the ongoing crisis.

Conclusion

The 2026 conflict in Lebanon has triggered a severe humanitarian emergency affecting millions of people. With displacement rapidly increasing, healthcare systems collapsing, and humanitarian funding falling far short of required levels, grassroots organizations are playing a critical role in providing life-saving support.

The Doria Feminist Fund’s Emergency Response Grants offer flexible and rapid funding designed to empower feminist collectives responding to the crisis on the ground. By strengthening local organizations and supporting vulnerable communities, the initiative seeks to ensure that humanitarian responses remain inclusive, community-driven, and responsive to the needs of those most affected by the conflict.

For further inquiries about the initiative, interested partners may contact the Doria Feminist Fund directly.

For more details about the Grant, click Here.

Discover more  similar opportunities here. 

The post Emergency Funding for Feminist Groups in Lebanon: Doria Feminist Fund Responds to 2026 Humanitarian Crisis appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: develoPPP Ideas Competition 2026: Funding of Up to €2 Million for Projects in Developing Countries
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Organizations and companies working to create sustainable impact in developing and emerging markets can now apply for the develoPPP Ideas Competition, a funding initiative that supports innovative business projects contributing to global development. The develoPPP programme, supported by the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ), provides financial and technical assistance to companies […]

The post develoPPP Ideas Competition 2026: Funding of Up to €2 Million for Projects in Developing Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations and companies working to create sustainable impact in developing and emerging markets can now apply for the develoPPP Ideas Competition, a funding initiative that supports innovative business projects contributing to global development.

The develoPPP programme, supported by the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ), provides financial and technical assistance to companies that combine business investment with measurable development impact.

Through this programme, eligible projects can receive between €100,000 and €2,000,000 in funding to scale solutions that improve livelihoods, strengthen markets, and support sustainable economic growth.

What Is the develoPPP Programme?

The develoPPP programme promotes partnerships between the private sector and development actors to implement projects that align with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

It supports companies investing in developing and emerging economies that want to expand their operations while contributing to social, economic, and environmental progress.

In addition to financial support, selected projects benefit from implementation guidance, local expertise, and access to development networks, helping turn promising ideas into real-world impact.

Minimum Eligibility Requirements

To apply for funding through the develoPPP programme, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Your company is registered in the EU, EFTA, or a country on the OECD-DAC list

  • You have a long-term business interest in the target country

  • Your project contributes to development outcomes aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)

Funding Opportunities Under the develoPPP Programme

The programme offers two main funding tracks designed to support companies at different stages of growth.

1. develoPPP Classic

develoPPP Classic is designed for established companies planning to expand their operations in developing or emerging countries through projects that generate measurable development benefits.

Key features:

  • Public funding of up to €2,000,000

  • Technical and financial support for project implementation

  • Focus on sustainable investments with development impact

  • Suitable for companies expanding their presence in emerging markets

This track supports projects that strengthen local economies, supply chains, skills development, and sustainable industries.

Apply NOW

2. develoPPP Ventures

develoPPP Ventures targets innovative start-ups and young companies that already operate in developing or emerging markets and are ready to scale their business models.

Key features:

  • Early-stage investment of up to €100,000

  • Matching funds model

  • Focus on scalable solutions with strong development impact

  • Designed for startups entering a growth phase

This funding is particularly useful for startups developing innovative solutions in areas such as climate, digital services, agriculture, and inclusive economic development.

Apply Now

Priority Sectors

Projects supported through the develoPPP programme often operate in sectors that contribute to sustainable development, including:

  • Climate and renewable energy

  • Transport and infrastructure

  • Digital economy and technology

  • Health and healthcare systems

  • Education and skills development

  • Sustainable agriculture and food systems

These sectors align closely with international development priorities and initiatives such as the EU Global Gateway strategy.

Why Apply for develoPPP Funding?

Beyond financial support, selected projects benefit from a range of advantages:

  • Access to development finance expertise

  • Implementation support and advisory services

  • Strong local and international partnerships

  • Opportunities to scale solutions with measurable development impact

The programme helps companies bridge the gap between commercial investment and sustainable development, making it easier to scale impactful initiatives.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 31 March 2026

Organizations planning to invest in developing markets and deliver measurable impact are encouraged to prepare their project proposals and apply before the deadline.

APPLY NOW OR GET MORE INFO HERE

Final Thoughts

If you are building a business or development project in emerging markets that aims to create jobs, strengthen local economies, and support sustainable growth, the develoPPP Ideas Competition offers an important opportunity to access catalytic funding and strategic support.

With funding of up to €2 million, this programme can help turn promising ideas into scalable solutions that benefit both businesses and communities.

Looking for more funding opportunities, fellowships, and scholarships worldwide?
Visit Opportunities For Youth (OFY):
https://opportunitiesforyouth.org

The post develoPPP Ideas Competition 2026: Funding of Up to €2 Million for Projects in Developing Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: EU Delegation Traineeship 2026 at the African Union (Funded Opportunity for Young Graduates)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Young graduates interested in international relations, diplomacy, and EU–Africa cooperation can apply for the Funded Traineeship at the EU Delegation to the African Union 2026. The traineeship offers hands-on experience supporting the Political, Press and Information (PPI) Section of the EU Delegation in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. Key Details Programme: EU Delegation to the African Union […]

The post EU Delegation Traineeship 2026 at the African Union (Funded Opportunity for Young Graduates) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young graduates interested in international relations, diplomacy, and EU–Africa cooperation can apply for the Funded Traineeship at the EU Delegation to the African Union 2026. The traineeship offers hands-on experience supporting the Political, Press and Information (PPI) Section of the EU Delegation in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia.

Key Details

  • Programme: EU Delegation to the African Union Traineeship

  • Location: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia

  • Host: European Union Delegation to the African Union

  • Duration: Up to 6 months

  • Tentative Start Date: 1 May 2026

  • Funding: Monthly grant for living expenses

  • Application Deadline: 3 April 2026

  • Eligibility: Young graduates currently residing in Ethiopia

Selected trainees will gain practical experience working within a European Union diplomatic mission, supporting policy research, communications, and cooperation initiatives between the European Union and the African Union.

Benefits of the Traineeship

Participants selected for the programme will receive:

  • A monthly grant to support living expenses

  • Experience working in a EU diplomatic mission

  • Exposure to EU–African Union policy and international cooperation

  • Opportunities to contribute to high-level policy discussions and international events

⚠️ Note: Travel, visa, accommodation, insurance, and other personal expenses must be covered by the trainee.

Traineeship Responsibilities

Selected trainees will support the Political, Press and Information Section in several areas:

Policy Research and Analysis

  • Conduct research on political and cooperation issues

  • Prepare summaries of meetings and policy papers

  • Support work related to mobility, migration, youth, economic integration, and multilateral relations

Communication and Media Support

  • Draft press releases, speeches, project fiches, and factsheets

  • Produce social media content, communication materials, and short videos

  • Assist in visibility and strategic communication initiatives

Event Coordination

  • Support organisation of internal and external events

  • Prepare reports and documentation

  • Attend meetings and prepare minutes

Policy and Cooperation Support

  • Contribute to the follow-up of the 2025 EU–AU Ministerial Conference

  • Support coordination of the Team Europe Communication Community

  • Assist the Policy and Cooperation Section when required.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Currently residing in Ethiopia

  • Hold a Master’s degree in:

    • Political Science

    • International Relations

    • Economics

    • Law

    • Social Sciences or related field

  • Have less than one year of professional experience after graduation

  • Be fluent in English

  • Demonstrate strong analytical, drafting, and communication skills

  • Experience using design and communication tools such as Canva

  • Strong interest in African Union politics, governance, and economic integration

Applicants should also demonstrate initiative, reliability, and willingness to contribute ideas.

Important information to read before applying

Before applying you are requested to examine the dedicated website as well as the general eligibility criteria for a paid traineeship (Article 8 of the Decision ADMIN(2017)28 – Paid traineeship).

Each selected trainee will receive a monthly grant to cover living expenses. All costs related to travels, visa, insurance, accommodation must be borne by the trainee.

Applications from non-eligible candidates will not be considered. After the evaluation of all applications, shortlisted candidates will be contacted and invited for an interview. Unsuccessful candidates will be notified by email. In case the selected candidates are not able to present the required documents, their application will be rejected.

The traineeships can only be offered in compliance with the local legislation as well as the legislation of the country of origin. 

 

Email
DELEGATION-AFRICAN-UNION-VACANCIES@eeas.europa.eu

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit:

  1. Europass CV
    https://europass.cedefop.europa.eu
    (CVs in other formats will not be accepted)

  2. Motivation letter / cover letter

  3. Completed application form

How to Apply

Candidates must apply via email.

Email:
DELEGATION-AFRICAN-UNION-VACANCIES@eeas.europa.eu

and send the following documents:

  • A detailed standard Europass curriculum vitae (CV) https://europass.cedefop.europa.eu/ (CVs in other format will not be considered)
  • a cover letter describing why you want to participate in a EU traineeship
  • and an application form

Email Subject Line:
Funded Traineeship – Political Press and Information Section

Selection Process

After reviewing applications:

  • Shortlisted candidates will be invited for an interview

  • Unsuccessful applicants will be notified by email

  • Selected candidates must provide all required documentation before final confirmation.

Traineeships are offered in compliance with local legislation and the legislation of the trainee’s country of origin.

Source : https://www.eeas.europa.eu/delegations/african-union-au/funded-traineeship-young-graduates-eu-delegation-african-union_gl

Looking for more scholarships, fellowships, and internships worldwide?
Visit Opportunities For Youth (OFY):
https://opportunitiesforyouth.org

The post EU Delegation Traineeship 2026 at the African Union (Funded Opportunity for Young Graduates) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded Journalism Fellowship 2026 to Cover the Global Environment Facility Assembly in Samarkand, Uzbekistan
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Journalists from low- and middle-income countries have a unique opportunity to report on one of the world’s most significant environmental governance events through a fellowship offered by the Internews Earth Journalism Network (EJN). The program will support selected reporters to attend and cover the Eighth Global Environment Facility (GEF) Assembly in Samarkand, Uzbekistan. The fellowship […]

The post Fully Funded Journalism Fellowship 2026 to Cover the Global Environment Facility Assembly in Samarkand, Uzbekistan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Journalists from low- and middle-income countries have a unique opportunity to report on one of the world’s most significant environmental governance events through a fellowship offered by the Internews Earth Journalism Network (EJN). The program will support selected reporters to attend and cover the Eighth Global Environment Facility (GEF) Assembly in Samarkand, Uzbekistan.

The fellowship is designed to strengthen journalism on environmental finance, climate policy, and global sustainability negotiations by enabling reporters to directly observe and report on high-level discussions shaping the future of global environmental action.

Applications for the fellowship are open until March 31, 2026.

Overview of the Fellowship

Internews Earth Journalism Network is offering fellowships to five journalists from low- and middle-income countries to attend and report from the Eighth Global Environment Facility Assembly, which will take place in Samarkand, Uzbekistan from May 30 to June 6, 2026.

The fellowship aims to improve global reporting on environmental financing and policy decisions by providing journalists with training, mentorship, and direct access to decision-makers attending the Assembly. Participants will gain firsthand insights into negotiations and policies that influence climate action, biodiversity protection, and sustainable development around the world.

Through this initiative, journalists will be able to produce impactful stories that explain how international environmental funding and governance decisions affect their home countries and local communities.

About the Global Environment Facility

The Global Environment Facility (GEF) is a multilateral environmental fund that supports developing nations in meeting the goals of several international environmental conventions.

Since its establishment in 1991, the GEF has provided more than $27 billion in grants and helped mobilize an additional $155 billion for environmental projects worldwide.

These funds support initiatives related to climate mitigation, biodiversity protection, sustainable land use, and pollution control. The GEF works closely with major global agreements such as:

  • Convention on Biological Diversity

  • United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change

  • Stockholm Convention on Persistent Organic Pollutants

  • United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification

  • Minamata Convention on Mercury

The GEF family of funds includes several specialized financing mechanisms such as:

  • Least Developed Countries Fund (LDCF)

  • Special Climate Change Fund (SCCF)

  • Global Biodiversity Framework Fund (GBFF)

  • Nagoya Protocol Implementation Fund (NPIF)

  • Capacity-building Initiative for Transparency Trust Fund (CBIT)

These funding programs support projects that protect water resources, strengthen agriculture and food security, improve health outcomes, reduce disaster risks, and safeguard fragile ecosystems.

About the Eighth GEF Assembly

The GEF Assembly is the governing body of the organization and includes representatives from 186 member countries. It meets periodically to review and approve major policy decisions, operational strategies, and amendments affecting global environmental funding.

The 2026 Assembly in Samarkand will bring together more than 1,000 delegates, including:

  • Government ministers

  • Environmental leaders and experts

  • Business representatives

  • Civil society organizations

  • Indigenous community leaders

  • Officials from international organizations and GEF agencies

Participants will discuss key issues such as:

  • Climate adaptation strategies

  • Private sector engagement in environmental action

  • Financing mechanisms for sustainable development

  • Partnerships to address global ecological challenges

The Assembly will feature nine high-level roundtables, multiple side events, field trips, and a Civil Society Forum scheduled for June 3.

Fellowship Activities and Training

Selected fellows will receive preparation and mentoring before and during the conference.

Prior to the Assembly, EJN will provide resources and reporting tools to help fellows prepare for the event. Once in Samarkand, fellows will participate in specialized activities designed to enhance their coverage of environmental negotiations.

These activities include:

  • Orientation sessions on key issues discussed at the Assembly

  • Daily briefings on ongoing discussions

  • Interviews with high-level officials and experts

  • Mentorship from an experienced environmental journalist serving as an EJN trainer

  • Collaboration and networking with other international fellows

The trainer will help participants understand complex policy discussions and provide feedback to support high-quality reporting throughout the event.

Story Production and Publication

Fellows may produce stories in several formats, including:

  • Print journalism

  • Online reporting

  • Radio broadcasts

  • Television or multimedia reports

Stories may be published in any language, but fellows must provide an English summary of their work. Additionally, at least one story must be translated into English so it can be shared with a broader international audience.

While fellows maintain full editorial independence, published work must acknowledge support from EJN by including the following statement:

“This story was produced as part of a reporting fellowship to the Eighth GEF Assembly supported by Internews’ Earth Journalism Network.”

Fellows are also free to publish their stories first through their affiliated media outlets. However, EJN and its partners retain the right to edit, republish, broadcast, and distribute the content.

Fellowship Benefits

The fellowship provides comprehensive financial and logistical support for selected journalists.

Covered expenses include:

  • Round-trip economy-class airfare

  • Accommodation in Samarkand

  • Meals during the fellowship period

  • Travel insurance

  • Local transportation

  • Rail transport between Tashkent and Samarkand if necessary

  • Visa fee reimbursement

  • Press accreditation support

  • Logistical assistance throughout the trip

Journalists selected for the fellowship are responsible for arranging their own visas and ensuring they meet all travel and attendance requirements.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a professional journalist working for or representing an established media organization in a low- or middle-income country

  • Have prior experience reporting on environmental issues

  • Experience covering climate, biodiversity, environmental negotiations, or environmental finance is an advantage

  • Demonstrate strong English language proficiency

  • Be available to arrive in Samarkand by May 31, 2026 and remain for the entire Assembly

  • Provide a letter of support from an editor or supervisor confirming that the outlet will publish or broadcast the resulting stories

  • Submit recent examples of relevant journalistic work

Applicants of all ages are welcome, though preference will be given to journalists aged 35 and under.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on several factors, including:

  • Proven experience reporting on environmental and climate topics

  • A clear and well-developed reporting plan for the Assembly

  • Potential audience reach of the applicant’s media outlet

  • A formal relationship with an established media organization

  • Diversity in geography and media types among selected fellows

Applicants must include a signed letter of support on official media letterhead confirming their outlet’s commitment to publish or broadcast the resulting stories.

Application Process

Interested journalists must complete their application through the fellowship portal.

To apply:

  1. Click the Apply Now button on the fellowship page.

  2. Log in to your existing account or create a new account if you do not have one.

  3. Complete the application form and upload all required supporting documents.

  4. Save drafts if necessary and return later to finalize the submission.

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications at least one day before the deadline to avoid technical issues.

Late submissions will not be considered.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the fellowship is:

March 31, 2026 (11:59 PM Pacific/Niue Time)

Successful applicants will be notified by mid-April 2026.

This fellowship provides a valuable opportunity for journalists to gain direct access to global environmental policy discussions while producing impactful reporting that informs audiences about climate finance, environmental governance, and sustainable development initiatives worldwide.

Discover more Fellowship opportunities here. 

The post Fully Funded Journalism Fellowship 2026 to Cover the Global Environment Facility Assembly in Samarkand, Uzbekistan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNFPA Global Internships — Paid Opportunities for Students and Graduates in Johannesburg, Copenhagen & Global Internship Roster
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Are you looking for a paid UN internship in international development? Applications are now open for several UNFPA Internship Opportunities in 2026, offering students and recent graduates the chance to gain hands-on experience working with the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA). These internships provide valuable exposure to global development, humanitarian programmes, youth engagement, and supply […]

The post UNFPA Global Internships — Paid Opportunities for Students and Graduates in Johannesburg, Copenhagen & Global Internship Roster appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you looking for a paid UN internship in international development? Applications are now open for several UNFPA Internship Opportunities in 2026, offering students and recent graduates the chance to gain hands-on experience working with the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA).

These internships provide valuable exposure to global development, humanitarian programmes, youth engagement, and supply chain operations, while allowing interns to work alongside experts addressing issues such as reproductive health, gender equality, population data, and youth empowerment.

If you want to start a career in the United Nations system, these internships can be a strong stepping stone.

Why Apply for a UNFPA Internship?

The UNFPA Internship Programme is designed to provide talented students and young professionals with real-world experience in international development and UN operations.

Benefits of UNFPA Internships

Selected interns may receive:

  • Monthly stipend (paid internship)

  • Opportunity to work within the United Nations system

  • Hands-on experience in programme management, research, policy, and operations

  • Mentorship from experienced UN professionals

  • Exposure to global development and humanitarian programmes

  • Networking opportunities with professionals across the UN and international NGOs

These internships are highly valuable for anyone pursuing careers in international development, global health, policy, research, humanitarian work, or public administration.

UNFPA Internship Opportunities 2026

Below are the currently available UNFPA internships and internship rosters open for applications. Deadline varies with some deadlines in December 2026

1. Programme Coordination Unit Internship – UNFPA ESARO

The Programme Coordination Unit Internship is based in the East and Southern Africa Regional Office (ESARO) in Johannesburg.

Interns will support programme coordination activities, research, reporting, and project management related to UNFPA programmes in the region.

Location: Johannesburg, South Africa
Office: UNFPA East and Southern Africa Regional Office (ESARO)
Contract Type: Internship
Deadline: 20 March 2026

Responsibilities may include

  • Supporting programme planning and coordination

  • Conducting background research and data analysis

  • Assisting with documentation and reports

  • Supporting regional programme initiatives and partnerships

APPLY NOW 

2. Youth Team Internship – UNFPA ESARO

This internship focuses on youth engagement and youth-led development initiatives across East and Southern Africa.

The Youth Team Internship offers an excellent opportunity for candidates interested in youth policy, advocacy, and youth empowerment programmes.

Location: Johannesburg, South Africa
Office: UNFPA East and Southern Africa Regional Office (ESARO)
Contract Type: Internship
Deadline: 20 March 2026

Key areas of work

  • Youth engagement and youth-led initiatives

  • Policy and programme support

  • Research on youth development and demographic issues

  • Communications and stakeholder engagement

APPLY NOW 

3. UNFPA Global Internship Roster 2026

UNFPA also maintains a Global Internship Roster, allowing qualified candidates to be considered for multiple internship opportunities across UNFPA offices worldwide.

Successful applicants may be matched with internship openings across country offices, regional offices, and headquarters.

Location: Multiple locations worldwide
Contract Type: Internship
Application Deadline: 31 December 2026

Benefits of joining the roster

  • Access to multiple UNFPA internship opportunities

  • Possibility of placements in different global offices

  • Exposure to various programme areas including gender equality, population data, reproductive health, and humanitarian response

APPLY HERE 

4. Supply Chain Internship Roster – Copenhagen

This internship roster is managed by the UNFPA Supply Chain Management Unit (SCMU) in Copenhagen.

The supply chain unit supports the procurement and distribution of essential reproductive health commodities and humanitarian supplies worldwide.

Location: Copenhagen, Denmark
Unit: Supply Chain Management Unit (SCMU)
Contract Type: Internship
Application Deadline: 31 December 2026

Interns may gain experience in

  • Global supply chain management

  • Procurement and logistics

  • Health commodity distribution

  • Data analysis and operational support

APPLY HERE 

Who Can Apply for UNFPA Internships?

To qualify for the UNFPA Internship Programme, applicants typically must:

  • Be enrolled in a university degree programme (Bachelor’s, Master’s, or PhD) or have graduated within the past year

  • Demonstrate strong academic performance

  • Have an interest in international development, population issues, gender equality, or humanitarian work

  • Possess strong communication and research skills

  • Be proficient in English (knowledge of additional UN languages is an advantage)

Why UNFPA?

The United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) works in more than 150 countries to improve reproductive health, promote gender equality, and empower young people.

Through its programmes, UNFPA supports initiatives such as:

  • Ending preventable maternal deaths

  • Ending unmet need for family planning

  • Ending gender-based violence and harmful practices

  • Supporting youth leadership and demographic research

Interning with UNFPA provides an opportunity to contribute to these global priorities while building a career in the international development sector.

Application Deadlines

  • Programme Coordination Unit Internship (Johannesburg): 20 March 2026

  • Youth Team Internship (Johannesburg): 20 March 2026

  • Global Internship Roster: 31 December 2026

  • Supply Chain Internship Roster (Copenhagen): 31 December 2026

Final Thoughts

The UNFPA Internships 2026 offer an excellent opportunity for students and graduates to gain professional experience with the United Nations while contributing to meaningful global initiatives in youth development, reproductive health, gender equality, and humanitarian response.

Because these opportunities are paid internships within the UN system, they are highly competitive. Applicants are encouraged to prepare strong CVs, academic transcripts, and motivation statements highlighting their interest in global development and UNFPA’s mission. For more jobs and internships at UNFPA, please visit:
https://www.unfpa.org/jobs

Looking for more global internships, fellowships, and scholarships?
Explore hundreds of opportunities at Opportunities For Youth (OFY):
https://opportunitiesforyouth.org

The post UNFPA Global Internships — Paid Opportunities for Students and Graduates in Johannesburg, Copenhagen & Global Internship Roster appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Stanford Venture Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded Trip to Stanford University)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Stanford Venture Fellowship 2026 is a fully funded one-week fellowship in the Stanford area, California, bringing together 10 exceptional fellows from around the world to explore entrepreneurship, innovation, and the Silicon Valley ecosystem. The program welcomes students and early professionals aged 14+ who want to gain firsthand exposure to startups, venture capital, research, and […]

The post Stanford Venture Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded Trip to Stanford University) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Stanford Venture Fellowship 2026 is a fully funded one-week fellowship in the Stanford area, California, bringing together 10 exceptional fellows from around the world to explore entrepreneurship, innovation, and the Silicon Valley ecosystem.

The program welcomes students and early professionals aged 14+ who want to gain firsthand exposure to startups, venture capital, research, and the Stanford innovation community.

Key Details

  • Host: Stanford Venture Fellowship

  • Location: Stanford Area, California, USA

  • Program Duration: 1 Week

  • Expected Dates: May 2026

  • Number of Fellows: 10 participants worldwide

  • Funding: Fully Funded (Travel reimbursed)

  • Eligibility: Open to applicants aged 14+ worldwide

  • Participants: High school students, university students, graduate students, and early professionals

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows will receive a fully funded experience, including:

  • Travel reimbursement from anywhere in the world

  • Accommodation in the Stanford area

  • Access to structured sessions, workshops, and discussions

  • Networking with Stanford students, founders, and faculty

  • Exposure to Silicon Valley startups and venture capital

  • Connection with a global cohort of fellows

Additional logistical details will be shared with selected fellows closer to the program.

About the Stanford Venture Fellowship

The Stanford Venture Fellowship (SVF) is a one-week in-person program created by Stanford students, alumni, and mentors.

The fellowship aims to bring ambitious individuals from around the world into the Stanford and Silicon Valley ecosystem, allowing them to learn directly from founders, researchers, venture capitalists, and innovators.

Participants will engage in workshops, mentorship sessions, discussions, and startup visits designed to provide insight into how innovation is built within one of the world’s most influential technology hubs.

What Fellows Will Experience

During the fellowship week, participants will:

  • Stay in the Stanford area throughout the program

  • Participate in structured workshops and discussions

  • Visit startups and innovation spaces in Silicon Valley

  • Interact directly with Stanford researchers and founders

  • Collaborate with a small cohort of global fellows

The program is designed to simulate what it feels like to live and learn inside the Stanford entrepreneurial ecosystem.

Example Fellowship Week

Sunday – Arrival

  • Arrival and check-in

  • Welcome dinner and introductions

Monday – Entering the Ecosystem

  • Opening session on the Stanford ecosystem

  • Campus walk and orientation

  • Workshop on building in ambitious environments

Tuesday – Founders & Startup Building

  • Founder conversations

  • Startup visits

  • Cohort working sessions

Wednesday – Research and Venture Capital

  • Faculty conversation

  • Venture capital insights session

  • Palo Alto exploration

Thursday – Mentorship and Skills

  • Technical or strategy workshop

  • Mentorship roundtables

  • Speaker dinner

Friday – Program Synthesis

  • Fellow presentations

  • Ecosystem discussions

  • Closing dinner

Saturday – Departure

  • Final gathering

  • Departures

The final schedule will be shared with selected fellows.

Fellowship Origins

The Stanford Venture Fellowship grew out of selective initiatives within the Stanford ecosystem.

Members of the team have previously led and contributed to programs including the Stanford Cowell Fund, Stanford’s student-led quantitative investment fund, which runs a highly selective analyst program, as well as initiatives connected to ASES (Stanford’s entrepreneurial society).

After several years of running similar initiatives within the United States, the program is now expanding globally to welcome fellows from around the world.

Industry Engagement

Past events organized by members of the Stanford Venture Fellowship team have included individuals from organizations such as:

  • OpenAI

  • Google

  • Tesla

  • Microsoft

  • Y Combinator

  • Jane Street

  • Goldman Sachs

These engagements provide fellows exposure to leaders in technology, finance, and entrepreneurship.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Must be 14 years or older

  • Open to applicants from any country

  • Students and early professionals are encouraged to apply

Eligible participants include:

  • High school students

  • Undergraduate students

  • Graduate students

  • Early-career professionals

Applicants under 18 may be able to attend with parent or guardian accompaniment, with travel support provided where appropriate.

Why You Should Apply

The Stanford Venture Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to:

  • Experience Silicon Valley innovation firsthand

  • Connect with global entrepreneurs and researchers

  • Build entrepreneurship and leadership skills

  • Expand your global professional network

  • Learn inside the Stanford ecosystem

With only 10 fellows selected worldwide, the program is highly competitive.

How to Apply

Applications will open soon. Click here 

Interested applicants can sign up to receive a notification when applications become available.

Still have questions? Visit our FAQ.

Partnership inquiries: partners@svfellow.com

For more scholarships, fellowships, and global opportunities, visit:
https://opportunitiesforyouth.org

The post Stanford Venture Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded Trip to Stanford University) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations Internet Governance Forum (IGF) Fellowship Programme 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland (United Nations)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Internet Governance Forum (IGF) Fellowship Programme 2026 is now open for applications. This United Nations fellowship opportunity allows professionals from developing countries to work with the IGF Secretariat at the United Nations Office in Geneva, Switzerland and gain hands-on experience in Internet governance, digital policy, and multistakeholder global cooperation. The fellowship is administered by […]

The post United Nations Internet Governance Forum (IGF) Fellowship Programme 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland (United Nations) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Internet Governance Forum (IGF) Fellowship Programme 2026 is now open for applications. This United Nations fellowship opportunity allows professionals from developing countries to work with the IGF Secretariat at the United Nations Office in Geneva, Switzerland and gain hands-on experience in Internet governance, digital policy, and multistakeholder global cooperation.
